Activity Status.description: The status of an activity - one of an enumerated set of values.

Actors.description: Actors or Solution Roles related to this element.

Additional Properties.description: Additional Properties  allow arbitrary properties not defined in the type definitions to be added to any referenceable element.

Agreement End Date.description: Date when the agreement expires or was terminated, in ISO 8601 format.

Agreement Start Date.description: Date when the agreement becomes effective, in ISO 8601 format.

Category.description: A user-defined category for the element, used to group related elements for display or search purposes.

Collection Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the collection.

Confidence.description: A percent confidence in the proposed adding of the member.

Confidence Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, level_identifier: int}. level_identifier uses ConfidenceLevel enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, AD_HOC=1, TRANSACTIONAL=2, AUTHORITATIVE=3, DERIVED=4, OBSOLETE=5, OTHER=99.

Confidentiality Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, level_identifier: int}. level_identifier uses ConfidentialityLevel enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, INTERNAL=1, CONFIDENTIAL=2, SENSITIVE=3, RESTRICTED=4, OTHER=99.

Content Status.description: The lifecycle status of an element.

Criticality Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, level_identifier: int}. level_identifier uses CriticalityLevel enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, MARGINAL=1, IMPORTANT=2, CRITICAL=3, CATASTROPHIC=4, OTHER=99.

Display Name.description: The common name of an element.

Effective From.description: The beginning of when an element is viewable.

Effective Time.description: The time at which an element must be effective in order to be returned by the request.

Effective To.description: The ending time at which an element is visible.

Element Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the element being referenced.

Entitlements.examples: Allowed to use within the organization.
Entitlements.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing entitlements.

Expression.description: An expression describing a membership, relationship or classification.

Extended Properties.description: A dictionary of properties that extend the properties of an entity type.

External Source GUID.description: The unique identifier of an external source.

External Source Name.description: The name of an external source

For Duplicate Processing.description: Flag indicating if the request is to support duplicate processing.

For Lineage.description: Flag indicating if the request is to support lineage.

Glossary Term.description: Term that provides meaning to this field.

GUID.description: A unique identifier - typically of an element in this context.

Impact Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, severity_identifier: int}. severity_identifier uses ImpactSeverity enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, LOW=1, MEDIUM=2, HIGH=3, OTHER=99.

Is Own Anchor.description: A flag indicating if the element is its own anchor or is anchored to a different element.

Journal Entry.description: A text entry into a journal.

Label.description: A label used to identify or categorise a relationship link.

Merge Update.description: A flag indicating if the update should be a merge or replace.

Namespace.description: The namespace that qualifies the names of elements within this asset or data structure.

Notes.description: Notes and observations about the element.

Obligations.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing obligations.

Parent at End1.description: A flag indicating if the parent is at end1 of the relationship

Parent Relationship Attributes.description: A dictionary of relationship attributes to establish the parent relationship.

Prime Word Classification.description: Classification from 0438. Applied to Referenceable. Marker classification (no attributes) indicating the element is a prime word in a naming standard.

Purpose.description: The purpose of this collection — a short description of why it exists or what it is used for.

Purposes.description: A list of purposes for this project (array<string>). Note: distinct from Collection.purpose (string, singular) which is a separate attribute on Collection Base.

Qualified Name.description: The unique, text name of an element.

Request ID.description: A user provided or system generated request id for a conversation.

Resource Description.description: Description of the resource or how it is being used. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Resource Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the supporting resource in a ResourceList relationship.

Resource Properties.description: Additional properties needed to use the resource. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Resource Use.description: Describes how the resource is being used. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Restrictions.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing restrictions.

Retention Classification.description: Classification from 0422. Structure: {status_identifier: int, confidence: int, steward: str, steward_type_name: str, steward_property_name: str, source: str, notes: str, basis_identifier: int, associated_guid: str, archive_after: date, delete_after: date}. basis_identifier uses RetentionBasis enum: UNCLASSIFIED=0, TEMPORARY=1, PROJECT_LIFETIME=2, TEAM_LIFETIME=3, CONTRACT_LIFETIME=4, REGULATED_LIFETIME=5, TIMEBOXED_LIFETIME=6, OTHER=99.

Scope.description: Scope of the definition or element.

Security Tags.description: Optional security tags for security processing.

Start Date.description: Date at which the license or certification takes effect.

Status.description: Repository or element header status for an element.

Steward.description: The identifier of the steward responsible for the element.

Steward Property Name.description: Property name used to identify the type of the steward.

Steward Type Name.description: The type name of the steward element.

Summary.description: A short summary of the element's meaning or purpose.

Usage.description: The usage guidance for this element — how it is intended to be used in context.

Usage Measurements.description: A dictionary of property:value pairs describing usage measurements.

User Defined Content Status.description: A user defined content status = only valid if content status is OTHER.

User Defined Status.description: A user-defined status value. Only valid when the primary status is set to OTHER.

Watch Resource.description: Whether the parent entity should receive notifications about changes to this resource. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Zone Membership.description: Zones scope visibility of elements to different users.

Absolute Uncertainty.description: The absolute margin of error for numeric values in this data value specification (in the same units as the value).

Aliases.description: Alternative names for this data field, used in different systems or contexts.

Allow Duplicate Values.description: If true, the data class allows duplicate values in the data field.

Average Value.description: A typical or average value for the data class, used in data quality assessments.

Containing Data Class.description: The data class that this data class is composed within (DataClassComposition relationship).

Data Class.description: The data class that specifies the valid values or patterns for this data field (DataValueDefinition relationship).

Data Patterns.description: Regular expressions or patterns that describe the format of valid values for this data class.

Data Type.description: The data type of the field or value specification (e.g. string, int, date, boolean).

Default Value.description: The default value assigned to this field or data class when no value is supplied.

Grain Assigned Fields.description: The data fields assigned to define the grain of this data grain entity.

Grain Statement.description: A human-readable statement describing the granularity of the data grain (e.g. 'one row per customer per day').

Granularity Basis.description: The basis on which the granularity is defined (e.g. time period, geographic region, customer segment). UML type: string — no enum defined in Egeria open types.
Granularity Basis.Journal Entry: The basis on which the granularity is defined, e.g. time period, geographic region, customer segment. Stored as a string (UML type: string).

In Data Dictionary.description: The data dictionary that contains this data structure.

In Data Field.description: The data field that this element is nested within (NestedDataField relationship).

In Data Specification.description: The data specification that contains this data structure.

In Data Structure.description: The data structure that contains this data field (MemberDataField relationship).

In Data Value Specification.attr_labels: In Data Class; In Data Grain
In Data Value Specification.description: The data value specification that this element is a sub-specification of (DataValueHierarchy relationship).

Interval.description: The time interval in milliseconds between data captures for time-based data grains.

Is Case Sensitive.description: If true, values for this data class or field are case-sensitive.

Is Deprecated.description: If true, this element has been deprecated and should not be used in new designs.

Is Nullable.description: If true, the field may hold null values.

Length.description: The maximum number of characters or digits allowed in the field.

Less Granular Data Grain.description: A data grain that is less granular than this one (i.e. covers a broader scope or longer time period).

Link Relationship Type Name.description: The open metadata type name of the relationship used in a LinkedDataField connection.

Linked Data Field 1.description: The first data field in a LinkedDataField peer relationship.

Linked Data Field 2.description: The second data field in a LinkedDataField peer relationship.

Match Property Names.description: The names of the properties used when matching values against this data value specification.

Match Threshold.description: The confidence threshold (0-100) required for a value to be considered a match for this data value specification.

Maximum Cardinality.description: The maximum number of times this field may appear in the containing data structure (-1 means unbounded).

Minimum Cardinality.description: The minimum number of times this field must appear in the containing data structure.

Minimum Length.description: The minimum number of characters or digits required in the field.

Name Patterns.description: Regular expressions or patterns that match valid names for this data structure or field.
Name Patterns.Journal Entry: Alternative name patterns for the element — often expressed as regular expressions.

Namespace Path.description: The namespace path that qualifies the element's name within a larger naming hierarchy.

Ordered Values.description: If true, the values in this field are ordered (i.e. sequence matters).

Position.description: The ordinal position of the data field within its containing data structure.

Precision.description: The number of significant digits after the decimal point for numeric fields.

Relative Uncertainty.description: The relative margin of error for values in this data value specification, expressed as a percentage (0-100).

Sample Values.description: Representative example values that illustrate the expected content of this data class or field.

Sort Order.description: The sort order for values in this field. Valid values from DataItemSortOrder enum: UNKNOWN, UNSORTED, ASCENDING, DESCENDING, OTHER.

Specializes Data Value Specification.description: The parent data value specification that this one specializes or refines.

Specification.description: A formal or technical specification string that describes the valid values or format for this data value specification.

Specification Details.description: Additional key-value details that extend the formal specification for this data value specification.

Units.description: The unit of measure for numeric values in this field or specification (e.g. metres, kg, USD).

Value List.description: An enumerated list of valid values for this data class.

Value Range From.description: The lower bound of the valid value range for this data class.

Value Range To.description: The upper bound of the valid value range for this data class.

Create Data Dictionary.description: Creates or updates a data dictionary — an organized collection of defined data fields serving as a knowledge base of preferred data definitions. Implemented as a Collection with the DataDictionary classification.

Create Data Specification.description: Creates or updates a data specification — a collection describing the data requirements for a project or initiative. Its members are typically data structures. Implemented as a Collection with the DataSpec classification.

Create Data Structure.description: Creates or updates a data structure — a collection of data fields that defines the structure for a data source.

Create Data Field.description: Creates or updates a data field — a named, typed element within a data structure. Supports nesting via In Data Field and data class assignment.

Create Data Class.description: Creates or updates a data class — a description of the values that may be stored in data fields. Supports composition via Containing Data Class and specialization via Specializes Data Class. Can be used to configure quality validators and data field classifiers.

Create Data Value Specification.description: Creates or updates a data value classification which represents the characteristics of a data value. DataClass and DataGrain are subtypes.

Create Data Grain.description: Creates or updates a data grain — a defined granularity of data that can be associated with a data field or data lens.

Link Data Field.description: Links or unlinks two data fields via the LinkedDataField relationship, with an optional relationship type name to describe the nature of the association (e.g. ForeignKey, DerivedFrom).
